name: Dental Records & Consent (DBA / AHPRA aligned)
description: Reference policy aligned with the Dental Board of Australia Code of Conduct.

data.text: Dental records & consent — DBA / AHPRA aligned
Records are clear, contemporaneous, and detailed enough for another practitioner to continue care.
Adult patient records are retained for at least 7 years from the last contact.
Records for patients aged under 18 are retained until the patient is 25 years old, or 7 years from last contact, whichever is later.
Tooth charting uses an explicit notation system (FDI is standard in AU); restorations, caries, mobility, and missing teeth are recorded for every full exam.
Periodontal status is recorded at every full exam.
Treatment items are coded against the Australian Schedule of Dental Services & Glossary.
Informed consent is documented for treatment plans, especially invasive procedures.

clauses.title: Records sufficient for handover
clauses.body: You must keep records detailed enough for another practitioner to continue care.

clauses.title: 7-year adult retention
clauses.body: Adult records must be retained at least 7 years from last contact.

clauses.title: Child retention to age 25
clauses.body: Records for patients under 18 must be retained until they are 25 or 7 years from last contact, whichever is later.

clauses.title: Tooth charting recorded
clauses.body: Every full exam must include tooth charting using an explicit notation system.

clauses.title: Periodontal status recorded
clauses.body: Every full exam must record periodontal status.

clauses.title: Schedule item codes used
clauses.body: Treatment items must be coded against the Australian Schedule of Dental Services & Glossary.

clauses.title: Treatment consent documented
clauses.body: You must document informed consent for treatment plans, especially invasive procedures.
